title: rubus schmidelyanus subsp. borreri
intro: Rubus schmidelyanus subsp. borreri, also known as the Borrer's bramble or wild raspberry, is a subspecies of the Rubus schmidelyanus plant, a type of thorny shrub that produces edible fruit and is native to Europe and parts of Asia. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Rubus schmidelyanus subsp. borreri include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and act as an antioxidant. The plant can be prepared as a herbal remedy in various forms, including teas, infusions, and jams, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
